rules.rule: Treat plugin entrypoints, manifests, schemas, and runtime configuration contracts as compatibility-sensitive. Flag removals or incompatible changes unless the pull request clearly documents a breaking change.
Review TypeScript, Python, and C++ integration points carefully. Changes that cross language boundaries must keep serialization formats, field names, and calling conventions aligned.

Prefer source changes over generated outputs. Flag committed build products, vendored dependencies, large binaries, or machine-generated files unless they are explicitly required for release or test fixtures.

Be strict about subprocess execution, shell command construction, filesystem access, network calls, and secret handling in agents or plugins. Flag untrusted input flows, unsafe defaults, and missing validation.

files.description: High-level repository overview for the plugin collection.
